title: Neural Processes, Live
tagline: A neural net that learns 'priors over functions' the way a Gaussian process does — but amortised
summary: Click context points onto a 1-D axis and a Conditional Neural Process predicts the rest with uncertainty bands that bow out where data is missing — exactly like a GP, but the kernel is replaced by a learned encoder. Compare the CNP to a real GP fit on the same context. See where the latent (LNP) and attentive (ANP) variants buy you something. Lab-aligned for sensor regression with a small calibration set.
highlights: Click context points onto a 1-D axis; a pre-trained CNP and a GP both predict the rest live, side-by-side.
Hand-tuned CNP weights so the demo runs without an autograd library, with a separate panel that shows the encoder→aggregate→decoder pipeline.
Toggle between CNP, latent NP (samples whole functions) and an attentive variant (kernel-like attention over context).
Where each variant wins and the lab-aligned applications for small-data sensor / thermal regression.
